inline_comments.message: I think we switched to using Instant
a lot of the models code checks updates the content of the current key, if it's not null.  Should that be done here, too?
hmm.  How many records could this return?  may want to force a limit on the record count and maximum time window.  may want to require starting timestamp
wonder if we could use a different term than "Tracker"?  That term is already used within PAP with a different meaning

Suggestions and questions about this were raised on the wiki page and announced on standup, so a definitive solution would be uploaded for the review.
https://wiki.onap.org/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=103417291
I based this one on PdpPolicyStatus (something created out of existent objects) and that one doesn't have that validation, that's why here also is absent.
I updated the wiki with my thoughts.
don't think this needs to be unique
should probably have a secondary index on this
is this necessary? - we don't do it in the other cases where Instant is used
I'd rather not modify the authorativeConcept
should we limit it to something?
it's giving a longer offset on milis and making then not equals.
that was an atempt at limiting. if not informed when used, it would limit it to 50. that was suggested on the wiki page.
@committers: should we do a similar thing in the other classes?  or just modify this class to match the others?
still need to add an @Index annotation to the class
Never mind - I should have looked at the change before commenting!
should probably trim changedByUser here, too
just put this back to avoid nullpointer exception
is LOCAL_NAME_REGEXP an acceptable pattern for changedByUser (i.e., is it perhaps too restrictive)?
I did some checking and seems ok for usernames as well.

As suggested earlier, we need to think futuristic and name entities accordingly. We may include create/delete of policies via policy-api component later as well. Considering that the name should be generic enough for different actions. Something like "PolicyAudit"
Ideally we shouldn't have added the methods here. Better to create a new provider called "PolicyAuditProvider" and base the methods there (similar to PdpStatisticsProvider).
Do we have some constants file for such information?
The title doesn't match with the method parameters. I think it will return details of the provided policy name & version only.
It should be "ASC" or "DESC" ? You may want to show the latest record on the top?
Corresponding changes will be needed in the test classes as well.
If the table in future is supposed to save information like create/delete of policies, then PdpPolicyAudit doesn't sound right. Any reason for not going with "PolicyAudit"?

if we're going to use this to store general audit records, then perhaps we should eliminate these fields and replace them with a simple string describing the action that was taken?  (And maybe move it somewhere else, like models-pap?)  Or is it OK to have it focused solely on policies, in which case the pdpGroup & pdpType would become optional/unused for create/delete records?
maybe rename to this instead: PdpPolicyAuditIndex_timestamp
for code coverage, probably need a case where the DB has 60 records, to verify that it limits the number of records to the provided limit, and another test where it limits to the default of 50.
this line should be reverted to its original
I just assumed the Pdp was a required prefix. Then it probably doesn't belong on this package. Should move to models-base or models-pap?
based on the weekly meeting, we should also have a "max max" which limits how many records a user may request

models-pap will be my choice.

PDP is Policy Decision Point where the execution of policies are done. The audit is for actions taken by users using the API's provided by Policy Framework. So, it has no relation to the PDP itself. And should be completely generic. For more details, please refer - https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-policy-parent/en/latest/architecture/architecture.html#id1
I agree to keep it focused for policies and keep the pdpGroup & pdpType optional/unused for create/delete records. And have it in models-pap.
seems like we want to have optional start & end times for all of the retrieval methods

does "createAuditRecords" sounds better?
same for gets.
As discussed yesterday in weekly call, we need "toDate" as well.

I am not 100% sure but wondering if this will work? As Instant.EPOCH & Instant.now will represent the same date/time.
May be just pass the null values to dao.getFiltered so that it simply ignores this filter? As having them null means users don't need to use that filter.
Thoughts pls?
should validate the JpaPolicyAudit objects - see the other provider classes
this also will be limited on API. check Ram's comments on wiki.
this is not part of the changes anymore.
this is not part of the changes anymore. all moved to models-pap
once the annotations are added above, this method can be deleted
probably want to include a contains(...) to ensure it lists the invalid fields
any update or reply for this?

sorry, I thought I had replied. It only gives "key is null" even if I test with name or version null, so I don't think it's necessary. as for timestamp, it won't give an error back because sonar complains if I just leave it to accept null so I had to give the Instant.now() so it's never null.
